flashcards.definition: Ecological succession starting on bare substrate with no soil; very slow (centuries).
Succession after disturbance that leaves soil intact; faster than primary (decades).
First organisms to colonize bare substrate (lichens, mosses in primary; weeds/grasses in secondary).
The relatively stable ecosystem that develops at the end of ecological succession.
An event that disrupts an ecosystem (fire, flood, storm, volcanic eruption, human activity).
